source.title: High-throughput screening of spike variants uncovers the key residues that alter the affinity and antigenicity of SARS-CoV-2

initial_request.research_problem: Systematically identify key residues in full-length SARS-CoV-2 spike protein that alter ACE2-binding affinity and monoclonal antibody evasion using a mammalian cell surface-display platform.
initial_request.known_context: Prior DMS studies limited to RBD in yeast with different glycosylation; majority of S protein not investigated.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Prior DMS studies limited to RBD in yeast with different glycosylation; majority of S protein not investigated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Yeast-based DMS may not recapitulate human glycosylation and full-length S protein context.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mammalian cell surface-display of intact S variants better mimics authentic viral infection for studying ACE2 binding and mAb evasion.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop a mammalian cell surface-display platform for full-length S protein.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The glycosyl pattern in yeast is quite different from that in human cells, and the majority of the S protein has not yet been investigated.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to design a lentivirus-based surface-display system.

evidence.quote_or_span: the glycosyl pattern in yeast is quite different from that in human cells, and the majority of the S protein has not yet been investigated

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No mammalian display system for full-length S protein.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a construct that enables surface display and quantification of S variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Fusing S variant to mCherry via T2A in a lentivirus backbone will allow surface display and expression monitoring.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design lentivirus backbone with S variant fused to mCherry via T2A.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test the construct in mammalian cells.

evidence.quote_or_span: we fused it to mCherry fluorescence with a T2A linker in a lentivirus backbone (Fig. 1a)

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to systematically mutate full-length S to identify key residues.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Site-directed saturation mutagenesis of full-length S will mimic random viral mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design oligo pools for 1254 positions × 19 substitutions = 23,807 variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Library designed to cover all possible single amino acid substitutions.

evidence.quote_or_span: we used oligo pools (1254 × 19 = 23,807, each carrying a designed mutation) to mutate the full-length S variants at specific sites
We used site-directed saturation mutagenesis to mimic the random viral mutations that occur in the host

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to ensure library covers >96% of point mutations.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Optimizing PCR parameters will yield high-coverage lentivirus library.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Optimize template/primer dosage, PCR temperature, cycles, etc.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Generated a high-coverage lentivirus library containing nearly all point mutations (>96%).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Transduce cells at low MOI for single integration.

evidence.quote_or_span: we optimized the dosage of templates and primers, controlled the PCR temperature, amplification cycles, and other parameters, and finally generated a high-coverage lentivirus library, which contained nearly all the point mutations of the S protein sequence (> 96%)

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to quantify enrichment of each mutation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: PacBio SMRT sequencing will provide accurate mutation frequencies.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence by PacBio SMRT Sequel-II HiFi mode and calculate fold change (FC = proportion in sorted/unsorted).
evolution_trajectory.observation: CCS reads obtained with high accuracy; FC calculated for each mutation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Classify mutational effects and identify key residues.

evidence.quote_or_span: third-generation DNA sequencing (PacBio SMRT Sequel-II, HiFi mode)
The total obtained CCS reads were output by the sequencing company with the following parameters: min-passes 3--min-rq 0.99--min-length 100 (SNP accuracy >99%).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm individual mutants' affinity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Candidates with FC > 2 will show higher affinity than Δfurin-S.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate 20 candidates by individual ACE2 binding assays.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 19 showed higher affinity than Δfurin-S; 8 reached VOC-binding affinity level (Y369N, A372T, K386N, L390E, L390S, K386E, K386V, G413N).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Investigate role of N-glycosylation in affinity.

evidence.quote_or_span: 8 sites (Y369N, A372T, K386N, L390E, L390S, K386E, K386V, and G413N) even reached the VOC-binding affinity level
S5a–v and S6b), of which 8 sites (Y369N, A372T, K386N, L390E, L390S, K386E, K386V, and G413N) even reached the VOC-binding affinity level (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: N-glycan-related mutants identified among top hits.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Y369N, A372T, L390S, G413N introduce new N-glycan sites.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Express RBD variants and treat with PNGase F to confirm glycosylation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: RBD-Y369N and RBD-A372T completely glycosylated (3 N-glycans); RBD-L390S and RBD-G413N incompletely glycosylated (2-3 N-glycans).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test effect of N-glycan addition on affinity and mAb resistance in Delta/Omicron.

evidence.quote_or_span: RBD-Y369N and RBD-A372T were completely glycosylated by three N-glycans, while RBD-L390S and RBD-G413N were incompletely glycosylated, encompassing both three N-glycan and two N-glycan isoforms

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do these N-glycans enhance affinity and mAb resistance in variant contexts?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Adding N-glycans to Delta and Omicron S will increase affinity and mAb resistance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Introduce L390S and G413N into Delta and Omicron S and measure affinity and REGN10987 binding.
evolution_trajectory.observation: L390S and G413N improved affinity 1.37–1.50-fold and increased mAb resistance in vitro.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test pseudovirus infectivity of N-glycan mutants.

evidence.quote_or_span: L390S and G413N steadily improved the affinity in vitro: 1.37-fold and 1.50-fold for Delta; 1.37-fold and 1.28-fold for Omicron

It was found that S205F, Y453F, Q493A, Q493M, Q498H, Q498Y, N501F, and N501T showed a 3–12-fold increase in infectivity, of which Y453F, Q493A, and Q498Y exhibited at least a 10-fold resistance to mAbs REGN10933, LY-CoV555, and REGN10987, respectively.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: N-glycan mutants increase affinity in vitro.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does increased affinity translate to higher infectivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: N-glycan mutants will enhance pseudovirus infectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test pseudovirus infectivity of N-glycan mutants in 293T-ACE2-Furin cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: N-glycan mutants drastically lost infectivity (e.g., L390S 816-fold reduction) despite increased affinity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Contradiction prompts systematic pseudovirus testing of other high-affinity mutants.

evidence.quote_or_span: the corresponding pseudovirus greatly lost the ability to invade HEK293T cells overexpressing ACE2 and furin

Four N-glycan-related mutants (Y369N, L390S, L390T, and G413N) drastically lost infectivity (293-fold, 816-fold, 1438-fold, and 62-fold, respectively), while two mutants (A372S and A372T) on the N370 glycan site showed slightly lower (1.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some non-glycan mutations may enhance infectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Systematically test pseudovirus infectivity of top affinity-enhancing mutants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Eight mutants (S205F, Y453F, Q493A, Q493M, Q498H, Q498Y, N501F, N501T) showed 3–12-fold increased infectivity over WT.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze relationship between affinity, infectivity, and furin cleavage.

evidence.quote_or_span: eight S variants (S205F, Y453F, Q493A, Q493M, Q498H, Q498Y, N501F, and N501T) displayed even higher invasion ability than D614G, ranging from a 3–12-fold increase compared to that of WT

Notably, eight S variants (S205F, Y453F, Q493A, Q493M, Q498H, Q498Y, N501F, and N501T) displayed even higher invasion ability than D614G, ranging from a 3–12-fold increase compared to that of WT (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: High-risk mutants identified (e.g., Q493A, Q493M, Y453F, Q498Y, Q498H).
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do these mutants evade therapeutic mAbs?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations at key RBD residues will confer resistance to mAbs targeting those epitopes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform neutralization assays with commercial mAbs (LY-CoV555, REGN10933, REGN10987, VIR-7831).
evolution_trajectory.observation: Q493A/M resistant to LY-CoV555 (8–58-fold); Y453F, Q493A resistant to REGN10933 (48–606-fold); Q498Y/H resistant to REGN10987 and VIR-7831 (3–19-fold).

evidence.quote_or_span: Q493A and Q493M exhibited higher resistance to LY-CoV555 (Fig. 5c, 58-fold and 8-fold, respectively)
Y453F and Q493A obviously prevented the neutralization of REGN10933 (Fig. 5c, 606-fold and 48-fold, respectively)
Q498Y and Q498H exhibited strong evasion of REGN10987 and VIR-7831 (Fig. 5c, 19-fold and 3-fold, respectively)
5c, 606-fold and 48-fold, respectively); Q498Y and Q498H exhibited strong evasion of REGN10987 and VIR-7831 (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: mAb resistance confirmed for key mutants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do these mutants also evade polyclonal serum antibodies?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Q498Y may exhibit broader defense against serum antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test serum neutralization against Q498Y pseudovirus.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Q498Y exhibited broader defense against serum antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude with limitations and future directions.

evidence.quote_or_span: Q498Y also exhibited broader defense against serum antibodies (Fig. 5d)

validation_methods: Individual ACE2 binding assays for top candidates
success_verification.final_conclusion: Identified ten key RBD residues for ACE2 affinity; N-glycan mutants increase affinity but reduce infectivity; eight mutations increase infectivity 3–12-fold; Q493A/M, Y453F, Q498Y/H confer mAb resistance; Q498Y evades serum antibodies.
limitations: Recombinational effect of multiple sites is complex; next variant hard to predict
Further screening of Omicron sequence needed
In vitro findings may not fully recapitulate in vivo infection
